description: Companion Mode — privacy-first nearby travel-buddy matching and multi-itinerary planning, layered on top of a strictly local, no-social-graph solo app. Three phases: (1) local multi-itinerary planning, (2) async itinerary-based companion discovery + double-opt-in handshake + in-app realtime chat, (3) foreground-only geohash6-blurred nearby presence. Default-off, location always blurred, exact coordinates never leave the device. Source: tasks/prd-companion-mode.md.

userStories.title: Define Itinerary data model in TS core
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a typed Itinerary schema in core so itineraries pass consistently across the three layers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/core/src/companion.ts exporting Itinerary and ItineraryId (branded type)
Itinerary fields: id, ownerId, title, cityCode, startDate (ISO date), endDate (ISO date), experienceIds (ordered ExperienceId[]), note?, openToCompanions, createdAt (ISO 8601 UTC), updatedAt
Export from packages/core/src/index.ts
Dates use ISO day-granularity; timestamps use ISO 8601 UTC per project convention
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Mirror Itinerary in Swift and pass parity check
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Itinerary.swift mirroring the TS type so the parity guard passes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/ios/SoloCompass/Models/Itinerary.swift with fields aligned to the TS Itinerary
ItineraryId is a strongly-typed Swift wrapper (struct wrapper or validated typealias), not a plain String
Provide a sample factory for #Preview usage
pnpm parity:check passes
xcodebuild build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Itinerary local persistence (SwiftData)
userStories.description: As a user, I want my itineraries saved locally so closing the app does not lose them.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a SwiftData model + ItineraryStore (or extend the existing persistence layer) implementing create/read/update/delete
New itineraries default to openToCompanions = false
Unit tests cover CRUD in apps/ios/SoloCompass/Tests/
xcodebuild test passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Itinerary list and detail UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all my itineraries and open one so I can manage multiple travel plans.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Views/Companion/ItineraryListView.swift and ItineraryDetailView.swift
List shows title / city / date range / experience count per itinerary
Empty state has guidance copy via NSLocalizedString
Each view has a #Preview
xcodebuild build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in iOS Simulator (iPhone 17 Pro): create / view / delete itinerary looks and behaves correctly

userStories.title: Create / edit itinerary form
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create an itinerary with title/city/dates so I can start planning.
acceptanceCriteria: Create form with title, cityCode (chosen from existing cities), startDate, endDate
Form validation: endDate must not be earlier than startDate
After save the itinerary appears immediately in the list
All copy via NSLocalizedString
xcodebuild build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in iOS Simulator: create and edit flows work

userStories.title: Add experiences to an itinerary / migrate from favorites
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add an Experience to an itinerary and group existing favorites into itineraries so favorites are no longer one unstructured pile.
acceptanceCriteria: Experience detail view has an 'Add to itinerary' entry (pick existing or create new)
Itinerary detail view can reorder experienceIds via drag
Provide an 'Import from favorites' action moving selected favoritedExperiences into a chosen itinerary
Existing favoritedExperiences behavior is not broken (itinerary is additive, not a replacement)
xcodebuild build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in iOS Simulator: add / reorder / import

userStories.title: Sync itineraries to Supabase via outbox
userStories.description: As a user, I want itineraries to sync across devices so switching devices does not lose them.
acceptanceCriteria: Create infra/supabase/migrations/0003_companion.sql containing the itineraries table (this table only for now) with its indexes and RLS (self-all + read-open)
Reuse SyncService.enqueue outbox pattern, adding itinerary sync handling
last-write-wins merge aligned with existing updated_at + device_id tiebreak logic
When FF_COMPANION flag is off, only record to outbox and do NOT flush (aligned with existing FF_BACKEND_SYNC pattern)
Unit tests cover enqueue + merge
xcodebuild test passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Complete companion data models (TS + Swift + migration)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CompanionPost / CompanionRequest / CompanionProfile / Conversation / ChatMessage / CompanionReport landed across all three layers.
acceptanceCriteria: companion.ts adds the above types + branded IDs (CompanionPostId, CompanionRequestId, ConversationId, MessageId), plus CompanionMode and CompanionRequestStatus unions
Add CompanionProfile to packages/core/src/user.ts (all-optional, does not break existing User)
Create the corresponding Swift models and pass parity:check

Extend 0003_companion.sql with all remaining tables (companion_profiles, companion_posts, companion_requests, conversations, chat_messages, companion_reports, companion_blocks) + their indexes + reverse RLS policies + updated_at triggers
pnpm typecheck + pnpm parity:check + xcodebuild build all pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Companion profile settings + visibility switch (default off)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to set my companion profile (emoji/bio/languages) and visibility so I control who can see me.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Views/Companion/CompanionProfileView.swift
visibility has three levels: off (default) / itinerary_only / nearby_and_itinerary
When off, the user does not appear in any discovery list (enforced by both RLS and the Edge Function)
No real-photo upload — emoji / generated avatar only
All copy via NSLocalizedString
xcodebuild build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in iOS Simulator: profile saves and visibility toggles work

userStories.title: Mark an itinerary as 'open to companions'
userStories.description: As a user, I want to open an itinerary to companions so same-city same-period people can find me.
acceptanceCriteria: Itinerary detail view has an 'open to companions' switch writing openToCompanions
Enabling prompts for blurb + categories and generates a CompanionPost(mode=itinerary)
Disabling immediately takes down that itinerary's post (delete or set expired)
When visibility=off, enabling is blocked with a prompt to turn on visibility first
All copy via NSLocalizedString
xcodebuild build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in iOS Simulator: open/close and prompts

userStories.title: Companion discovery list via Edge Function
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see same-city open itineraries filtered by date/category so I can find suitable travel buddies.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Edge Function companion-discover: inputs city_code / mode / dates / categories; filter with service role + exclude blocked pairs; return only de-identified fields (handle / emoji / blurb / blurred distance bucket) — never exact coordinates
Create CompanionService.swift calling the function
Add Views/Companion/DiscoverListView.swift; cards show handle/emoji/blurb/dates/categories
List returns no exact coordinates
Empty-state copy + cold-start prompt ('No one in this city is open to companions yet')
All copy via NSLocalizedString
xcodebuild build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in iOS Simulator: discovery list renders and filters

userStories.title: Send / accept / decline companion requests (double opt-in)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to request a companion post and only connect after they accept so contact requires mutual consent.
acceptanceCriteria: Discovery card can 'send request' (with ice-breaker message <=200 chars) writing CompanionRequest(pending)
Add RequestInboxView.swift showing received pending requests with accept/decline
When both sides are accepted, the backend creates a Conversation (via Edge Function or DB trigger for consistency)
After decline / withdraw no conversation is created and the two no longer disturb each other
xcodebuild build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in iOS Simulator with two anonymous accounts: full handshake (test DB + two devices acceptable)

userStories.title: In-app lightweight realtime chat (Supabase Realtime)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to text an accepted travel buddy inside the app so we can arrange to meet.
acceptanceCriteria: ChatService.swift subscribes to the chat_messages Realtime channel by conversation_id
Views/Companion/ChatView.swift: text input, message bubbles, ordered by sent_at
Only conversation participants can read/write (RLS enforces; client double-checks)
Messages <=1000 chars; plain text only (no images/attachments)
After reconnect, fetch messages missed while offline (sent_at > lastSeen)
xcodebuild build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in iOS Simulator: bidirectional realtime send/receive

userStories.title: Report and block
userStories.description: As a user, I want to report or block someone so I can protect myself from harassment.
acceptanceCriteria: Report can be started from any post / request / message / profile (pick reason + optional detail)
After blocking: the other person disappears from all my discovery lists, cannot send me requests, and any existing conversation is frozen
Reports write to companion_reports (a user cannot read others' reports)
Blocks write to companion_blocks; the discovery Edge Function excludes blocked pairs bidirectionally
xcodebuild build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in iOS Simulator: report submission and block takes effect

userStories.title: PresenceService — foreground-only, manual switch, geohash6 broadcast
userStories.description: As a user, I want to manually enable 'nearby companions' broadcasting only a blurred location in the foreground so I can find nearby people with controlled privacy.
acceptanceCriteria: Create PresenceService.swift that starts only when the user actively enables it in the UI
Subscribe to LocationService.currentLocation, convert to geohash6 (~600m), and report a CompanionPost(mode=nearby, geohash6) every N minutes
When the app enters background OR the user turns the switch off, immediately stop reporting and take down the nearby post
Exact coordinates never leave the device (compute geohash6 locally then discard the precise value)
Do not request Always authorization; WhenInUse is sufficient
Unit tests: switch toggling, background stop, coordinate->geohash6 conversion correct
xcodebuild test passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: LocationService coarse geohash seam
userStories.description: As a developer, I want LocationService to expose a coarse (geohash6) read seam so PresenceService never touches precise GPS management.
acceptanceCriteria: LocationService adds a coarseGeohash6 computed property / publisher derived from the existing currentLocation
Existing precise-location and geofencing logic is unchanged (regression-test existing region behavior)
geohash6 encoding has unit tests (known coordinate -> known hash)
xcodebuild test passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Map companion layer (default off)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the nearby companion grid on the map so I can intuitively discover people around me.
acceptanceCriteria: CompassMapView adds an optional 'companion layer' switch (default off)
When on, call companion-discover(mode=nearby) and render as geohash6 grid centers + 'within ~600m' blur — never draw exact points
Turning the layer off returns the map to its current state with no companion elements
When visibility=off or Presence is not enabled, the layer entry is disabled with an explanation
xcodebuild build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in iOS Simulator: layer toggle and blurred-grid rendering

userStories.title: Auto-expiry and cleanup
userStories.description: As a system, I want to auto-clean expired nearby posts and stale data so lists do not show ghost users.
acceptanceCriteria: nearby post expires_at <= 2h; discovery query filters expires_at > now() (RLS already includes this)
Scheduled cleanup (Supabase scheduled function or query-time filter) removes expired posts
pending requests that time out (e.g. 7 days) auto-set to expired
Unit/integration test verifies expired items do not appear in discovery results
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: reporterWeight downranking + discovery sort
userStories.description: As a system, I want to use the existing trust score to sort companion discovery and downrank low-score users so abusers are harder to find.
acceptanceCriteria: Edge Function discovery results are weighted-sorted by reporter_weight descending
Repeated reports trigger a reporter_weight decrease (rule documented)
Users below a threshold do not appear in the discovery list
Unit tests cover the sort and the threshold
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Safety copy, minor protection, and emergency clauses
userStories.description: As a product, I want safety guidance and a disclaimer/report channel shown at companion entry and before meeting so we meet compliance and the user-safety baseline.
acceptanceCriteria: First-time companion opt-in shows safety notice + consent terms (no precise location before meeting, meet in public, report entry point)
Minor-protection statement / age confirmation
Update App Store privacy manifest (PrivacyInfo): purposes for location, user content, contact info
UGC (blurb/bio/messages) has basic min-length / sensitive-word filtering
Legal terms marked as a human-confirmation open item (not decided by development)
All copy via NSLocalizedString
xcodebuild build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: One-tap full off / data deletion
userStories.description: As a user, I want to turn off all companion features and delete my companion data in one tap so I can fully exit the social layer.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings item 'Turn off companions and delete my companion data': set visibility=off, take down all posts, delete profile / itinerary open flags
After turning off, the app experience is identical to never having enabled companions
Deletion cascades (profile/posts/requests/conversations associated cleanup)
xcodebuild build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in iOS Simulator: no companion traces remain after turning off
